name: MACD Histogram CHOP Transition Breakout
edge_hypothesis: The highest alpha in our dataset comes from TRANSITIONAL regimes (EMA200 Vortex v2 trans PF=4.321, Vortex v3a trans PF=3.886). The transition from ranging to trending is the most profitable moment to enter. MACD histogram zero-cross is a momentum acceleration signal — when MACDh crosses from negative to positive, it means momentum is building and accelerating, not just present. Combined with CHOP > 50 (which confirms the market is currently range-bound), this creates a transition-specific entry: we enter the moment momentum BEGINS to build inside a range. This is fundamentally different from the existing MACD strategies (PPR 3.862) which use the macd_confirmation template with EMA trend filters. Those strategies enter IN trends; this strategy enters AT the transition FROM range TO trend. MACD histogram has never been tested as a zero-cross transition detector — only as a trend confirmation signal. This tests MACD histogram as a 4th independent transition-detection mechanism alongside Vortex (directional cross), Ichimoku TK (median-price cross), and SMC (structural breaks).

variants.name: macdh_chop_transition_v1_8to1
variants.description: Core thesis: MACD histogram zero-cross as transition signal + CHOP ranging gate. 8:1 R:R. MACDh crossing above 0 = momentum accelerating bullishly while CHOP > 50 confirms range-bound market = entering the transition moment. Only 2 entry conditions.

components.notes: MACD histogram — zero-cross signals momentum acceleration/deceleration. Used here as transition detector, NOT trend confirmation. Crosses above 0 = bullish momentum building.
Choppiness Index > 50 = range-bound market confirmed. Gate ensures we enter during ranges, catching the transition TO trend.
ATR-based position sizing and 8:1 R:R framework.

variants.name: macdh_chop_transition_v1_relaxed_8to1
variants.description: Relaxed CHOP threshold: > 45 instead of > 50. This captures slightly less choppy markets — early-stage ranges or transitional zones where the range hasn't fully developed. Tests whether the MACDh zero-cross signal has edge even in borderline ranging conditions. May increase trade count at the cost of some ranging purity.

components.notes: Relaxed CHOP threshold (> 45) for broader ranging capture.

variants.name: macdh_ema200_transition_v1_8to1
variants.description: Alternative regime filter: EMA_200 replaces CHOP as trend direction gate. MACDh crossing above 0 while price is above EMA_200 catches momentum building in the direction of the major trend. This is a trend-pullback variant rather than range-breakout variant — diversifies the hypothesis by testing MACDh zero-cross in a different regime context. EMA200 has amplified transitional alpha in prior tests (trans PF=4.321) but requires 1.0+ ATR stop for high-volatility zones.

components.notes: MACD histogram zero-cross — momentum acceleration signal used as transition detector.
EMA 200 as major trend direction filter. Known to amplify transitional alpha (trans PF=4.321 in prior tests).
ATR-based sizing. 1.5 ATR stop (above 1.0 minimum for EMA200-filtered entries).

indicator_roles.baseline: CHOP_14_1_100 (ranging gate) or EMA_200 (trend direction filter) — regime context for transition timing
indicator_roles.confirmation: MACDh_12_26_9 — MACD histogram zero-cross as momentum acceleration/transition detection signal (novel use vs traditional MACD confirmation)
indicator_roles.volume_volatility: ATR_14 — position sizing and R:R framework
